$description.$value: State-based color mapping for interaction elements. Interactive state colors for components. Both Lightness (Light/Dark) and Emphasis (High/Low) modes affect resolved values across all 4 columns. Do: Reference when authoring compiled interaction tokens that require emphasis resolution. Avoid: Do not assign directly to component tokens — use compiled S-tier tokens instead.

Default emphasis for interactive elements — buttons, links, and form controls where the interactive role must be clearly visible. Colors are saturated and fully contrasted to signal primary actions. Organized into standard_states (predefined interaction states) and contrast_levels (raw contrast steps for custom compositions). Do: Use for primary buttons, default call-to-action elements, and text links in content areas where a saturated interactive color is expected. Avoid: Do not use in headers, footers, or passive layout zones where saturated color would compete with surrounding content. Use Low emphasis instead.

Predefined interaction state colors for emphasis-high components — enabled, hover, focus, pressed, selected, visited, and disabled. Lightness mode is pre-resolved; Emphasis High is fixed. Use: Authoring compiled standard_states tokens for primary interactive components. Avoid: Custom contrast compositions — use the contrast_levels group instead.

$description.$value: Raw contrast-level colors for emphasis-high interaction. Provides fg, bg, and border at three contrast steps without state semantics. Use: Custom interaction compositions requiring direct contrast control not covered by the predefined states. Avoid: Named interaction states such as hover and focus — use the standard-states tokens instead.
